reasoning: The Manifold Market estimate on May 15 was 30%. Since then, TSLA shares have stabilized around the $340 level, buoyed by a substantial mid-May rally. A fall from $340 to below $200 represents a >40% decline. While TSLA historically experiences massive volatility (with implied volatility often hovering around 50-60%), achieving a 41% drawdown within the remaining ~7 months of 2025 carries an option-implied terminal probability of roughly 13-15%, making the 'probability of touching' $200 around 25-30%. Since the price hasn't dropped back toward its 52-week low ($214) and remains near the $340 mark heading into late May, the likelihood decays slightly compared to the mid-May 30% anchor. A conservative estimate of 28% captures both the substantial historical volatility of TSLA and the significant gap between current prices and the $200 threshold.
